name: zone-field-data
description: Field data names are used by FISH zone.field queries and correspond to values available through zone history. They include zone-based values and gridpoint-based values interpolated at requested positions.

notes: FLAC2D accepts only the x/y components plus magnitude form (no '-z' kinematic field).
'displacement-z' / 'velocity-z' / 'acceleration-z' are FLAC3D-only and rejected by both 'zone history' and 'zone.field.name' in FLAC2D.
Tensor values may require a quantity selector such as maximum, minimum, mean, or shear-maximum.
FLAC2D plane-strain workflows may still report out-of-plane stress values, but out-of-plane position/vector commands remain FLAC3D-specific.
'condition' is the constitutive-model state code field (NOT 'state' — that token is rejected by both 'zone history' and 'zone.field.name').
property and extra field queries require the relevant property or extra index to be set through the corresponding zone.field function.
Fluid fields require fluid configuration where applicable.
Thermal fields require thermal configuration where applicable.
'thermal-flux' is NOT a zone field name — use 'property-thermal' for thermal material constants and a thermal-specific FISH query for flux.

quantity_notes: Quantity selectors apply to tensor fields (stress, strain-rate, strain-increment, stress-effective) via the 'quantity' sub-keyword of zone history, e.g. 'zone history stress quantity von-mises position (0.5,0.5)'.

Quantity tokens can also be appended directly to the field name as a single concatenated form (e.g. 'stress-von-mises', 'stress-shear-maximum', 'stress-mean') — both forms validated against FLAC2D 9.0.

fish_patterns.name: Single-point query (FLAC2D)
notes: FLAC2D vector is 2D: vector(x, y). FLAC3D is vector(x, y, z).
